title: Polestar Margin Compression Pricing Pressure EV Market
text: Polestar Q1 2026 loss widened sharply to $383 million from $166 million as pricing pressure in Europe and U.S. tariffs squeezed margins despite 7% rise in deliveries. Gross margin swung to negative 3.2% from positive 10.3% in Q1 2025, dramatic deterioration in profitability. Company faces significant financial headwinds from intense pricing pressure within EV market, adverse U.S. and EU tariffs and unfavorable product mix. Polestar rolled out discounts to attract cautious buyers as U.S. tariffs compress margins and raise manufacturing costs. Company accelerating efforts to streamline business model, improve manufacturing efficiencies and become leaner. Product mix challenges show Polestar 3 vehicles accounted for 9% of sales while lower-priced Polestar 4 represented 67%, suggesting shift toward lower per-unit profitability models. Mitigation strategy involves manufacturing regionally where customers located, citing Volvo plant in South Carolina for U.S. market along with regional strategies for Asia and planned European Polestar 7 production.
